title: Reinforce small-bead and coalesced followup rules in planner prompt builder
description: Update `build_planner_prompt()` so the planner prompt itself repeats the sizing constraint for implementation beads and instructs the planner to emit shared followup beads when multiple related developer beads exist.
acceptance_criteria: `build_planner_prompt()` includes explicit guidance that implementation beads should stay narrowly scoped and under roughly 10 minutes each.
The prompt text tells the planner to create shared tester/review/documentation beads that depend on all related developer beads.
The prompt still preserves the required JSON output contract and top-level feature-root structure.

handoff_summary.completed: Added explicit planner prompt guidance in `src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py` that developer implementation beads must stay narrowly scoped, target roughly 10 minutes of work, split broader changes into dependent beads, and emit shared tester/documentation/review follow-up beads that depend on the related implementation set. Verified the file compiles with `uv run python -m py_compile src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py`.
handoff_summary.risks: Prompt wording now duplicates planner guardrail intent in code; downstream planner behavior still depends on how strictly the model follows prompt instructions. No tests were run per developer-agent guardrails.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low. Change is isolated to planner prompt text in `src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py`; downstream agents should treat prompt snapshots generated before this change as stale.

conflict_risks: Low. Change is isolated to planner prompt text in `src/codex_orchestrator/prompts.py`; downstream agents should treat prompt snapshots generated before this change as stale.

execution_history.summary: Updated `build_planner_prompt()` to restate narrow developer bead sizing and shared tester/documentation/review follow-up rules while preserving the existing planner JSON contract and feature-root structure.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
